content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Deberah Bringelson 
Assistant Treasurer for 
Senator Becky Morgan 
State Capitol 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Ms. Bringelson: 
January 5, 1990 
Re: 
Your Request For Informal Assistance 
Our File No. 1-89-593 
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided 
to you concerning the status of Taxpayers to Limit campaian 
Spending v. FPPC (Case No. B039177, Court of Appeal, Second 
Appellate Dist., Div. 3). 
Since our conversation of October 13, 1989, the Supreme Court 
has decided to grant the Commission's petition for review. 
Until 
the Supreme Court issues an opinion in the Taxpayers case, 
candidates are not subject to the limitations, including the off-
year ban on contributions, set forth in Proposition 68. 
It is 
anticipated that it will be several months before the final 
opinion is issued. 
If you have any questions, please feel free to call me at 
(916) 322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
By: 
Linda Moureaux 
Political Reform Consultant 
428 J Street, Suite 800 .. P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Linda Moreaux

Paid for by Friends of Becky Morgan 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Deberah R. Bringelson 
Assistant Treasurer for 
Senator Becky Morgan 
State Capitol 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Ms. Bringelson: 
October 20, 1989 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-593 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on October 17, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

sections.conclusion: Until the Supreme Court issues an opinion in the Taxpayers case, candidates are not subject to the limitations, including the off-year ban on contributions, set forth in Proposition 68.
sections.question_synthetic: What is the current status of contribution limitations and off-year contribution bans under Proposition 68 given the pending Taxpayers to Limit Campaign Spending v. FPPC Supreme Court petition?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Candidates are not currently subject to Proposition 68 contribution limitations, including off-year contribution bans, pending the California Supreme Court's decision on the case. The Court has granted the Commission's petition for review and a decision is expected within several months.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple letters dated October-January 1989-1990. Primary FPPC response is the January 5, 1990 letter from Linda Moureaux confirming prior telephone advice. No explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections found; conclusion extracted from letter body. Included acknowledgment letter and requester's confirmation letter for context.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What is the current status of contribution limitations and off-year contribution bans under Proposition 68 given the pending Taxpayers to Limit Campaign Spending v. FPPC Supreme Court petition?

CONCLUSION: Until the Supreme Court issues an opinion in the Taxpayers case, candidates are not subject to the limitations, including the off-year ban on contributions, set forth in Proposition 68.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises that pending the Supreme Court's decision in Taxpayers to Limit Campaign Spending v. FPPC, candidates are not subject to Proposition 68 contribution limitations. The document confirms prior telephone advice from October 13, 1989.
